excluded_defs.name: WithMessage

exploration_log.aha_moment: RuleBase.CreateValidationError reads component.ErrorCode with a null-coalescing fallback to the global resolver, so inserting a conditional on a new ErrorCodeProvider property before that gives clean delegate-based dynamic codes with no other changes needed.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could also have added a SetErrorCode(Func<>) method on RuleComponent mirroring SetErrorMessage(), but a plain auto-property is simpler and consistent with SeverityProvider.

alternatives: reuse ErrorCode with a sentinel value
key_decisions.decision: Add ErrorCodeProvider as a plain Func<> property on RuleComponent, checked before ErrorCode in CreateValidationError
key_decisions.reasoning: Mirrors the SeverityProvider pattern exactly; minimal API surface change, easy to discover

search_sequence.result: Found WithMessage(Func<T,string>) and WithMessage(Func<T,TProperty,string>) calling SetErrorMessage; found WithErrorCode(string) setting ErrorCode

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to add a new field to store the delegate
search_sequence.result: ErrorCode is a simple string property at line 154; SeverityProvider at line 149 shows the pattern for a Func property

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to find where ErrorCode is consumed to emit ValidationFailure.ErrorCode
search_sequence.result: CreateValidationError at line 328 reads component.ErrorCode with null-coalesce to global resolver; needs to check ErrorCodeProvider first

queries.justification: Rule compliance: describes only domain/business concepts (error code, delegate, validation failure, dynamic, instance-based) without any symbol names. Pre-implementation: a developer implementing dynamic error codes would search this before knowing which files to change.
queries.query_text: Allow error codes on validation rules to be computed dynamically from the validated object at runtime instead of being set as static strings

queries.justification: Rule compliance: 'codeProvider' and 'ErrorCodeProvider' appear literally in the added source code. Pre-implementation: a developer grepping for the pattern would look for these strings.

Rule compliance: names concrete symbol WithErrorCode with callers/implementors relationship. Pre-implementation: an agent would trace callers of WithErrorCode to understand the extension pattern.
queries.query_text: Callers of WithErrorCode and implementors of IRuleBuilderOptions that store error code in RuleComponent

queries.justification: Rule compliance: provides 3 explicit file paths from the solution. Pre-implementation: the task names DefaultValidatorOptions.cs and the agent would also look at RuleComponent.cs and RuleBase.cs as obvious related files.
Rule compliance: mixes domain concept (dynamic error code delegate) with 3 exact symbol names. Pre-implementation: natural query for the task goal using known symbols.

queries.query_text: Add WithErrorCode(Func<T,string>) and WithErrorCode(Func<T,TProperty,string>) overloads in DefaultValidatorOptions.cs storing delegate in RuleComponent.ErrorCodeProvider, invoked in RuleBase.CreateValidationError

solve_notes: Added `ErrorCodeProvider` property (a `Func<ValidationContext<T>, TProperty, string>`) to `RuleComponent` mirroring `SeverityProvider`. Added two `WithErrorCode` overloads in `DefaultValidatorOptions` for `Func<T, string>` and `Func<T, TProperty, string>`. Updated `RuleBase.CreateValidationError` to invoke `ErrorCodeProvider` first, falling back to the existing static `ErrorCode` and global resolver.
task_text:
The `DefaultValidatorOptions.cs` provides `WithErrorCode(string code)` to
set a static error code on a rule, but unlike `WithMessage()` there are no
overloads that accept a delegate to derive the code at validation time.
Add `WithErrorCode<T, TProperty>(Func<T, string> codeProvider)` and
`WithErrorCode<T, TProperty>(Func<T, TProperty, string> codeProvider)`
extension methods in `DefaultValidatorOptions.cs` that store the delegate
in `RuleComponent` and invoke it when building the `ValidationFailure`,
mirroring how `WithMessage(Func<T, string>)` works for error messages.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: WithMessage
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: reference implementation showing how delegate overloads of WithMessage store the factory in SetErrorMessage; upfront context prevents agent trying a different storage pattern

thrash_preventing_defs.name: SeverityProvider
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: exemplar showing how an existing Func<> provider property is stored and later consumed in CreateValidationError

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the three edited locations. An AI agent would also inspect the existing WithMessage overloads (to replicate the delegate storage pattern) and the SeverityProvider property (to see how an analogous Func<> property is already used in the same CreateValidationError method) — without seeing these, it might invent an incompatible storage mechanism and need to backtrack.
